persona.name: Trevor Lindqvist
persona.occupation: Junior support analyst at a B2B travel platform
persona.background: New to the tool and second-guesses himself constantly. The person who used to run the account just quit and left him holding it with no real handoff. He isn't sure what he's allowed to do versus what his own IT team should handle.

user_goals: Understand the current state of the account now that the previous admin has left
Find out who the listed contacts are so he knows who still has access
Get the onboarding restarted for the new teammates who never finished it
Book an onboarding session with the CS team to walk the new owners through setup
Have a note added to the account flagging that the prior champion departed and ownership moved to him

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: get_user_list
tools.description: List all users under a customer account with their status and last activity.

tools.name: get_onboarding_status
tools.description: Check onboarding completion rates and stuck points for an account's users.

tools.name: schedule_meeting
tools.description: Schedule a meeting between the customer and their CS team.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups. Do NOT use it to communicate with the customer (use send_customer_email) or to record a conversation that already happened (use log_customer_interaction).

tools.name: log_customer_interaction
tools.description: Record that a customer interaction ALREADY happened (call, email thread, meeting) on the account timeline for reporting. This is a logging action only — it does NOT send anything to the customer. To actually contact the customer, use send_customer_email.

parameters.name: summary
parameters.description: Short summary of what was discussed

initial_message: Um, hi, sorry to bother you. So the person who used to own our account here just left the company and now it's kind of fallen on me, and I honestly don't really know where things stand. Could you help me get a handle on it?

expected_state_changes.goal: Book an onboarding session with the CS team to walk the new owners through setup
Have a note added to the account flagging that the prior champion departed and ownership moved to him
